details: The postinstall hook (node setup.js) downloads and executes shell scripts from two remote hosts. The script runs cp.exec() to pipe shell from hxxp://example[.]com/payload[.]sh and hxxp://185[.]44[.]32[.]1/payload[.]sh directly into bash. It also reads the GITHUB_TOKEN environment variable. Despite comments claiming the code is simulated and safe, the exec calls are uncommented and executed at install time. The package description claims it is a security-testing demo but has no verifiable repository. Postinstall hook runs 'node setup.js' which executes two curl|bash commands to download and run remote payloads from example[.]com/payload.sh and 185[.]44[.]32[.]1/payload.sh. The setup.js script also reads the GITHUB_TOKEN environment variable and probes ~/.ssh/id_rsa (likely for exfiltration to the second-stage payload). The package is a two-stage downloader that fetches an external shell script upon installation.
